assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 for a foreign exchange exposure arising from an intragroup dividend receivable.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposures to changes in fair value of recognised assets or liabilities attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposures to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk, including highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.
